response.justification: Candidate is a solid senior full-stack engineer with ~13-14 years of experience, but the role demands a Director-level Principal Engineer with 15+ years and recognized AI expertise. Resume shows only recent, peripheral exposure to AI (MCP tooling at Naver) with no demonstrated AI products, platforms, ML depth, cloud architecture leadership, or advisory experience with strategic customers. No advanced degree, no evidence of entrepreneurial leadership inside large orgs at the scale required, and no track record of inspiring industry-level technical direction.
strengths: Broad polyglot full-stack background across web, mobile, embedded, and infra
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at Naver
Active community contributor — talks, workshops, open-source GIS platform
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML products, platforms, or recognized expertise required for the role
Experience level and scope are senior IC, not Director-level Principal/strategic advisor
Minimal cloud-architecture leadership and no evidence of advising strategic customers/partners

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise / shipped AI platforms
Seniority gap vs. Director-level Principal IC scope
Broad engineering versatility and recent MCP/agent work
